title: Coursetia greenmanii

This family consists of over 19,000 species, making it one of the largest and most diverse in the plant kingdom. Coursetia greenmanii is further classified within the subfamily Faboideae, which includes many of the most economically important legumes. The genus Coursetia itself is a relatively small group of flowering plants, with around 10-15 species found primarily in the Americas. As a member of this genus, Coursetia greenmanii shares certain characteristics with its relatives, such as compound leaves and showy flowers.
